objective: Establish a governed multi-user research access boundary before MCP, reports, cards, the Web App, or chat expose personal research state.
claim_ceiling: Accepted E2 Local/CI governed-research-access contract with stable package-root exports, release-bound sealed publication policy sets, entitlement-backed materialized-result authorization, administrator-only registered-replay request authorization, paired append-only decision/audit records, backward-compatible additive SQL bindings, frozen repair corpus, and a stable handoff for explicitly named consumers. This does not accept Gate 0, activate authentication or an identity provider, deploy browser or MCP access, retain private content, enable sharing or break-glass access, write replay requests, execute factors, Qlib, or replay, call live sources, bind a release, or claim Staging/Production readiness.

requirements: the #229 capability registration and A0 batch registration are merged to main before this prepared revision is accepted
#81 has accepted the actual-diff, merge-base, corpus-byte, integration-lease, command-evidence, and handoff enforcement required to activate queued batches
this preparation freezes the access corpus path and SHA-256 without changing implementation paths
the stable activation base remains 2fe3bc7e43c89b317caceeb4d627657cf7c50729 while implementation evidence records its exact current PR base and head
the independent reviewer remains assigned through terminal E2 acceptance
the active A0 integration lease authorizes only the authoritative architecture/export integration and the generic versioned Gate 0 successor support required to publish manifest v5
this preparation changes no implementation, preserves manifest-v4.json byte-for-byte, and leaves the accepted claim ceiling at E1
the E2 implementation must preserve every external blocker carried by Gate 0 v4 and must not claim Gate 0 acceptance
post-merge E0 review findings are classified as local contract bugs, rerun at E0, and included in the E1 tiny-corpus acceptance
issue #230 contains this manifest path and exact SHA-256
Delivery Governance CI passes offline validation and live GitHub parity

later_claim_dependencies: E2 freeze requires a separately valid Gate 0 v5 successor that binds the accepted access boundary into authoritative init.md and the stable contracts export without mutating frozen manifest-v4.json
#41 must publish an accepted E2 stable typed mart-read handoff before #42, #43, or #45 implement against this access handoff
#225, #226, and #227 must each publish their resolved implementation design before their conversation, document, or Web work is scheduled
#66 owns deployed authentication, entitlement, privacy, and consumer validation; this batch makes no Production claim
a named product/legal owner must approve retention, deletion, export, and break-glass policy before any user-content retention or exception is activated

corpus.environment: Local/CI checked-in synthetic authorization fixtures only
required_strata: two isolated tenants with positive owner reads and owner-only conversation and document records
standard and premium entitlements over two immutable publication classes plus a wrong-policy grant denial
administrator access to policy-permitted strategy/backtest results and non-content audit metadata only
administrator-only authorization to request an already registered replay definition when the policy explicitly permits that action
administrator denial for private conversation/document content
missing, invalid, expired, revoked, and forged role/tier authentication contexts
valid browser-session, delegated-MCP, administrator, and service-identity authentication contexts
cross-tenant guessed conversation and document object IDs with denial before any mart SQL, private-row lookup, or artifact retrieval

paired decision/audit identity, private-resource-ID redaction, and append-only grant, revocation, publication-policy-set, decision-grant, allowed-access, denied-access, and non-content audit histories
legacy nullable authorization-decision writes remain available during additive rollout while typed E2 decisions require a sealed policy set
policy rules are immutable after the append-only policy-set seal and unsealed sets cannot authorize typed E2 resources

source_ceiling: Checked-in typed and SQL fixtures only through E2; no network identity-provider call, credential, live source, data refresh, or external MCP client is used.

release_activation.reason: This batch creates only an authorization contract and private-state storage boundary. No ReleaseManifest, default route, schedule, consumer binding, or production identity provider may select it.

commands: uv run ruff check tools/check_gate0_candidate.py tools/check_delivery_governance.py libs/runtime/tests/test_gate0_candidate.py libs/runtime/tests/test_delivery_governance.py
negative_controls: missing, expired, revoked, cross-tenant, or client-supplied access identity reaches a mart query, artifact retrieval, or mutation
administrator reads private conversation or document content without separately approved break-glass evidence
grant, revocation, publication policy, or access audit row is updated or deleted instead of appended
private app rows bypass RLS or return after cross-tenant object-ID guessing
a contradictory resource privacy shape bypasses the publication policy for a materialized strategy or backtest result
an audit event and its authorization decision disagree on tenant, principal, event kind, or time, or administrator metadata exposes a private resource identifier or crosses tenants
ordinary app runtime reads administrator audit metadata or either immutable audit base table
AuthorizationService hides publication-policy, authentication-failure, or delegation-revocation inputs required by authorize_access
raw or staging access, arbitrary SQL, model-generated SQL, factor computation, Qlib invocation, or replay execution is introduced
AccessContext, tenant, principal, role, entitlement, or publication-policy metadata enters factor, BacktestDataGateway, DecisionSnapshot, ReplayEventStream, or Qlib types
a default route, release binding, scheduler, live credential, identity-provider request, or Production readiness claim is activated
the stable package root omits the exact governed-access symbols or ReleaseManifest gains an access binding
a materialized result is authorized without a matching publication rule and active entitlement grant, or a wrong-policy grant is recorded on an allow decision
an unsealed publication policy set authorizes a typed E2 resource, or a sealed policy set accepts another entitlement rule under the unchanged identity and content hash
a pre-E2 decision writer that omits the new nullable resource fields is rejected during the additive migration rollout or disappears from administrator audit metadata
a non-administrator or a policy without the explicit action authorizes a registered replay request
the frozen governance/gate0/manifest-v4.json bytes or its historical acceptance boundary are changed
the Gate 0 v5 successor drops, weakens, or marks accepted any external blocker inherited from v4
a Gate 0 v6-or-later successor accepts without loading its frozen predecessor commit and comparing immutable Git bytes
a terminal batch closes an issue or exempts accepted-evidence changes for an issue outside its capability_issues ownership

output.retention: E0/E1 evidence follows CI retention; the accepted E2 contract, handoff, policy identities, and audit schema are retained permanently.

invalidation: AccessContext identity grain, authentication method, action/resource vocabulary, publication-policy identity, RLS context protocol, append-only event semantics, retention policy, or corpus change creates a new batch revision and reruns affected rungs
a change to App-direct-mart, LLM/MCP typed-read, factor/backtest isolation, or source-budget constraints requires an init.md amendment before implementation
a release, route, identity-provider, credential, sharing, export, break-glass, or replay-execution requirement expands scope and requires a separate authorized batch

any change to frozen manifest-v4.json, a v5 predecessor artifact/hash, the generic Gate 0 version contract, init.md, or the stable package-root export invalidates E2 and requires a new successor candidate and handoff revision
a revoked grant or policy preserves historical audit/evidence but blocks new authorization decisions under that grant or policy
rollback: Before E2 implementation, revert this preparation revision and its expanded lease together. After E2, revoke the produced handoff, remove only manifest-v5.json and the generic successor support if no later candidate consumes it, and revert the init/export integration; never edit or delete manifest-v4.json. The additive app schema remains backward compatible, and authorization, grant, revocation, publication-policy, and audit records are never mutated or deleted.
